evidence: 7 tools all snake_case: search_terms, translate_term, check_terms, get_entries, list_collections, list_classifications, api_status (server.py)
No spaces/dots/special chars; docstrings carry use-case + scope caveats
Rich docstrings on search_terms/translate_term/check_terms with scope caveats and differentiation
gaps: Median description length ~90 chars (list_collections/api_status are one-liners), just under the 100-char bar
No formal <use_case> tag (contextual equivalent only)

evidence: Dual transport selectable via TERMDAT_MCP_TRANSPORT (server + __main__)
Tools are transport-agnostic; no direct request access
gaps: Config read directly from os.environ, not a pydantic-settings object
Module-global mcp and _client instead of a settings/lifespan-provided instance

evidence: Tools return self-contained results (TermEntry/TranslationHit), not pointers
gaps: check_terms iterates up to 25 terms sequentially; no asyncio.gather parallelisation

evidence: Server exposes Tools primitive only
gaps: No Resources or Prompts; no README justification for tools-only
Static vocabularies (list_collections/list_classifications) are Resource-migration candidates

evidence: All mandatory top-level files present (README.md, README.de.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, pyproject.toml)
src/ layout, tests/, .github/workflows/ (ci.yml + publish.yml) present
gaps: 7 tools live in a single server.py; catalog suggests a tools/ split for >5 tools
CI workflow named ci.yml, catalog names test.yml (cosmetic)

evidence: CHANGELOG.md in Keep-a-Changelog format; README has 'MCP Protocol Version' section; Dependabot active
gaps: protocolVersion is negotiated by the FastMCP SDK at the initialize handshake, not pinned in server code

evidence: Tool argument errors raised as ValueError -> FastMCP returns them as tool execution errors (isError), not JSON-RPC errors
api_status catches upstream failure and returns a structured reachable=false result
gaps: No explicit isError construction; relies on FastMCP default exception handling
Error-path test coverage is thin

evidence: api_status no longer forwards the raw exception text to the model (masked in this audit run)
No traceback.format_exc()/exc_info in any tool return
gaps: FastMCP 1.28.1 exposes no mask_error_details flag; raised ValueError/RuntimeError messages still reach the client as tool-error text

evidence: No print()/stdout writes in src/ (good for stdio hygiene, OBS-004)
gaps: No structured logging library (structlog/loguru) in dependencies; no logging at all
No per-call bound context (tool name, correlation id)

evidence: tests/test_client.py (respx-mocked) + tests/test_live.py (live-marked); live marker registered in pyproject; CI runs pytest -m 'not live'
gaps: 12 offline tests total across 7 tools (< 5 per tool)
No separate nightly/manual live-test workflow

evidence: README has all 8 mandatory sections incl. ASCII architecture diagram and Known Limitations; README.de.md parallel; CHANGELOG Keep-a-Changelog
gaps: No separate bilingual CONTRIBUTING.md (only a Contributing section in the READMEs)

evidence: Server is single-instance and not cloud-deployed (deployment: local-stdio; is_cloud_deployed=false)
gaps: SSE transport is offered but there is no sticky-session/shared-state design, session TTL or failover story

evidence: Single shared httpx.AsyncClient is created lazily and reused across calls (client.py _client()) — no per-call client
gaps: No FastMCP lifespan; the shared httpx client is never closed on shutdown (aclose() exists but is unwired)

evidence: Most tools are single upstream calls (<2s)
gaps: No ctx: Context injection anywhere; check_terms (up to 25 sequential calls) can exceed 2s with no progress reporting

evidence: stdio is the default transport; SSE is opt-in and not cloud-deployed
gaps: No CORS middleware configured for the SSE transport; Mcp-Session-Id is not explicitly exposed; allow_origins not restricted

evidence: All egress targets a single hardcoded trusted host (api.termdat.bk.admin.ch); no user-controlled hostnames
gaps: No DNS pinning; httpx resolves per request (TOCTOU-theoretical only, single trusted host)

evidence: Server runs locally via stdio by default; no container is shipped
gaps: No Dockerfile / k8s manifests, so non-root USER, readOnlyRootFilesystem, cap-drop, seccomp are not yet expressed

evidence: Language codes whitelisted (normalise_language); search fields whitelisted; empty/25-term guards; Pydantic-typed args and returns
gaps: max_results has no ge/le bound
No min/max_length on string args
strict=True / extra='forbid' not set on tool inputs

evidence: All requests go to the hardcoded BASE_URL/SPEC_URL (single host); no user-controlled URLs
gaps: Egress host is a module constant, not a frozenset allow-list with an assert_host_allowed pre-request check; no network-egress docs

evidence: check_terms returns verdict not_found + note; CheckResult.caveat states absence != incorrect
Documented exact-lookup design (no fuzzy fallback) fits the sensitive-lookup path
No secrets anywhere (unauthenticated API); no secret defaults in os.environ.get
/.env guarded by .gitignore
gaps: No gitleaks/trufflehog CI (low value: server has no secrets)

evidence: 7 tools (<=12); translate_term/check_terms are high-level use-cases, not 1:1 endpoint maps
All 7 tools annotated readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/openWorldHint via _READ_ONLY (server.py:30)
README documents read-only annotations
Every response Envelope carries source=ATTRIBUTION naming TERMDAT, BK and the licence caveat (models.py)
README documents the data source and licence
No print()/logging/stdout writes in src/ (grep clean); stdout stays clean for JSON-RPC
README declares Phase 1 (read-only); annotations match; phase-transition prerequisites documented
gaps: Roadmap lives in the README, not a separate file

evidence: Pydantic 2.7; explicit BaseModel return types; shared Envelope (source/provenance/retrieved_at); Literal types for provenance/verdict/kind
All egress targets the hardcoded HTTPS BASE_URL; no user-controlled URL/host -> no SSRF surface
Default transport is stdio; SSE only via TERMDAT_MCP_TRANSPORT; default start opens no TCP port
README documents local stdio primary + cloud separately
No pre/postinstall hooks; install shown transparently (uvx termdat-mcp)
PyPI OIDC Trusted Publishing (publish.yml) active
No API keys/credentials (unauthenticated public API); nothing to store
Default HOST is 127.0.0.1 (__main__.py:38); 0.0.0.0 is explicit opt-in and warns on stderr outside a container (_warn_on_public_binding)
README + SECURITY document the local/container differentiation
gaps: Remediated within this audit run (was 0.0.0.0 default)

evidence: Read-only, public-data server with no send/write capability -> at most one leg of the lethal trifecta
SECURITY.md documents read-only/no-write posture
No os.system/subprocess/shell=True/eval/exec/os.popen anywhere in src/ (grep clean)
auth_model=none: no user identity and no authenticated sessions to bind
gaps: N/A per profile — mirrors fedlex ADR 0002 (accepted, no-auth)
